---
title: Offline Auth Primitives
description: Client-first, offline auth continuity for JS and React
---

# Offline Auth Primitives

Use offline-auth primitives to keep local auth UX stable when connectivity is poor, without coupling Syncular to any specific provider.

Default model: client-first.

- Keep auth cache + lock policy locally.
- Keep Sync server auth strict (normal online auth for `/sync`).
- Clearing online session (logout) clears cached offline identity by default.

## Install

```bash
npm install @syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth @syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth-react
```

## Package split

| Package | Required? | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| `@syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth` | Yes | Offline session/identity cache, expiry checks, subject resolution, token bridge, lock policy state machine |
| `@syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth-react` | If React app | `useOfflineAuthState` + `useOfflineLockPolicy` hooks |

## Minimum setup (JS)

```typescript
import {
  createWebStorageAdapter,
  createTokenLifecycleBridge,
  loadOfflineAuthState,
  persistOnlineSession,
  resolveOfflineAuthSubject,
  saveOfflineAuthState,
} from '@syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth';
import { createHttpTransport } from '@syncular/transport-http';

// Wrap browser storage so offline-auth primitives can use a uniform interface.
const storage = createWebStorageAdapter(window.localStorage);
// Restore previously cached offline auth state (session/identity/last actor).
const offlineState = await loadOfflineAuthState({ storage, codec });

// Persist latest online session from your provider into the offline cache.
const next = persistOnlineSession({
  state: offlineState,
  session: authSession,
  getSessionActorId: (s) => s.userId,
  getExpiresAtMs: (s) => s.expiresAtMs,
});

// Save updated cache state to storage.
await saveOfflineAuthState(next, { storage });

// Resolve which subject the app should use right now.
// Precedence: valid online session -> offline identity -> last actor.
const subject = resolveOfflineAuthSubject({
  state: next,
  getSessionActorId: (s) => s.userId,
  getSessionTeamId: (s) => s.teamId,
});

// Bridge token lookup/refresh into Syncular transport auth lifecycle.
const tokenBridge = createTokenLifecycleBridge({
  resolveToken: async () => next.session?.value.accessToken ?? null,
  refreshToken: async () => auth.refreshToken(),
});

// Use bridge helpers for HTTP auth headers + refresh/retry behavior.
const transport = createHttpTransport({
  baseUrl: '/api',
  getHeaders: tokenBridge.getAuthorizationHeaders,
  authLifecycle: tokenBridge.authLifecycle,
});
```

## React add-on

```typescript
import {
  useOfflineAuthState,
  useOfflineLockPolicy,
} from '@syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth-react';

// React helper around load/save/update/clear for offline auth state.
const authState = useOfflineAuthState({ storage, codec });

// Local lock policy state (idle lock + activity tracking).
const lock = useOfflineLockPolicy({
  lockOnMount: true,
  idleTimeoutMs: 5 * 60_000,
  trackWindowActivity: true,
});
```

## PIN code example (React, app-specific)

Use the lock primitives to build your own PIN flow. The plugin does not own PIN UI, PIN storage, or PIN sync.

```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { useOfflineLockPolicy } from '@syncular/client-plugin-offline-auth-react';

type PinGateProps = {
  readStoredPinHash: () => string | null; // local-only storage
  hashPin: (pin: string) => Promise<string>; // app-owned KDF/hash
};

export function PinGate({ readStoredPinHash, hashPin }: PinGateProps) {
  // Local-only lock policy. This is UI/device protection, not server auth.
  const lock = useOfflineLockPolicy({
    lockOnMount: true,
    idleTimeoutMs: 5 * 60_000,
    maxFailedAttempts: 5,
    cooldownMs: 30_000,
    trackWindowActivity: true,
  });
  const [pin, setPin] = useState('');
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);

  const submit = async () => {
    // Verify against app-owned local PIN material (not synced via Syncular).
    const result = await lock.attemptUnlockAsync(async () => {
      const expectedHash = readStoredPinHash();
      if (!expectedHash) return false;
      const actualHash = await hashPin(pin);
      return actualHash === expectedHash;
    });

    if (result.ok) {
      setPin('');
      setError(null);
      return;
    }

    // Cooldown branch after too many failed attempts.
    if (result.reason === 'blocked') {
      setError('Too many attempts. Try again in a moment.');
      return;
    }

    setError('Invalid PIN');
  };

  if (!lock.state.isLocked) return null;

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>Unlock</h2>
      {/* Keep the input UX numeric, but final validation is app-owned. */}
      <input
        value={pin}
        onChange={(e) => setPin(e.target.value)}
        inputMode="numeric"
        autoComplete="one-time-code"
      />
      <button onClick={submit}>Unlock</button>
      {error ? <p>{error}</p> : null}
    </div>
  );
}
```

Notes:

- Keep PIN material local to each device.
- Do not sync PINs or PIN hashes through Syncular data tables.
- Use `forceUnlock()` only for explicit operator override flows.

If you involve the server, keep it limited to normal auth concerns:

- issuing normal session/access tokens
- returning policy config (for example lock timeout/cooldown defaults)

Keep `/sync` authentication strict and online-auth based.
